title: streptanthus lemmonii
intro: Streptanthus lemmonii, also known as Lemmon's jewelflower, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its vibrant yellow and orange flowers, and its roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The roots of Streptanthus lemmonii have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the roots of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal benefits, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
